model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Round of 16 · 6 July 2026 · Dallas. Final score: Portugal 0–1 Spain. Spain needed a moment of stoppage-time quality to break stubborn, well-drilled Portugal.

Two Iberian heavyweights, one quarter-final ticket, and ninety minutes that gave almost nothing away until the very last breath.

The goals, in sequence: 91′ Mikel Merino — 0–1. That is 1 goals in all, Portugal accounting for 0 and Spain 1.

The first half was shot-high and goal-shy; Mikel Oyarzabal spurned Spain's best early chance after Dani Olmo sent him clean through. Portugal sat deep, stayed compact, and looked to have earned extra time with a disciplined defensive display. But it fell to substitute Mikel Merino to snatch it at the death, his cool late strike settling a tie that had refused to break. In tournaments this tight, the difference between going home and going through is often a single decisive touch.

Momentum, as ever in the knockouts, proved a fragile and fleeting thing.

The expected-goals picture tightened around every set piece and transition.

Fine margins decided it, as they so often do at this stage of a tournament.

For the record, Spain advanced. Goal, team and minute for every event above are Merkle-proven on Solana devnet via TxLINE; the scorer names are attributed from public match reports and aligned to the verified goals by team and order. Nothing here rests on the commentator's word alone.
